title: Liftorium program resource
description: Versioned, app-ready program resource produced by the spreadsheet import workflow and consumed by the Android ProgramResourceLoader and the read-only Web review surfaces. Phase 2 (program-resources) defines the substantive content (exerciseCatalog, requiredReferences, programStructure with discriminated prescription targets, progressionRules) on top of the Phase 1 contract fields (schemaVersion, metadata, validationStatus, validationIssues, importAudit). Activation also requires the semantic validator in schema/semantics.ts; this schema only checks structural well-formedness.

schemaVersion.description: Monotonically increasing program-resource schema version. Phase 2 starts at 1. Bump to 2 when the resource uses programWeek.variantOf (week-level runtime alternates). Bump to 3 when the resource uses any of: percentTarget range form (percentMin+percentMax), prescriptionItem.restMaxSecondsHint, or prescriptionItem.warmupSetCount. Loaders that do not understand a higher schemaVersion must reject the resource.

validationIssues.description: Issues recorded by the import validator. Critical issues block activation per docs/architecture.md.

programId.description: Stable program identifier. Same across versions of the same program.

programVersionId.description: Stable identifier for this specific immutable program version. Never reused.

displayName.description: Human-readable program name shown in Android program library.

authorAttribution.description: Optional author/source attribution. Never includes source spreadsheet excerpts.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical resource content (metadata minus contentHash + exerciseCatalog + requiredReferences + programStructure + progressionRules + programDefaults). Excludes validationStatus, validationIssues, and importAudit so re-validating a resource never changes its content hash. ProgramResourceLoader (Phase 4) uses this to detect the 'same version ID, different content = conflict' case.

validationStatus.description: Aggregate activation gate. 'activatable' means no critical issues remain and ProgramResourceLoader will accept this resource as-is. 'pending_runtime_references' means the resource is structurally complete at import time, and the only remaining criticals are first-week reference.missing_first_week entries for runtime-pending training_max / one_rep_max references that the loader injects from the user profile at activation. 'blocked' means critical issues remain that the operator must iterate on. 'rejected' means the resource cannot be retried without re-import and is never activatable.

code.description: Stable machine-readable dot-namespaced code (e.g. 'construct.drop_set'). Never includes source excerpts.

message.description: Summarized issue description; never includes private source excerpts.

locationHint.description: Optional sheet/cell reference such as 'Week3!B12' to help the operator review. Must not include cell values.

sourceHash.description: SHA-256 of the source workbook. Synthetic fixtures may use the all-zero sentinel; private imports must use a real digest.

sourceFilename.description: Source workbook filename. Path components are stripped before storage.

importedAtUtc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the resource was finalized.

sourceKind.description: Provenance discriminator. 'synthetic' permits the all-zero sentinel hash and stub filename for fixtures and examples. 'private_import' marks a real operator spreadsheet and requires a real sourceHash.

schemaVersionUsed.description: Schema version the importer targeted. Must equal top-level schemaVersion (semantic check).

roundingOverride.description: Optional rounding override. Precedence (lowest to highest): programDefaults → block → exercise → prescription target. Phase 2 only validates shape; Phase 4/8 (program runner / training-max progression) enforce precedence at calculation time.

id.description: Stable canonical exercise id, kebab-case. Survives re-import per the exercise recognition precedence in docs/architecture.md.

family.description: Stats inclusion bucket; the four main-lift families feed e1RM trends per docs/architecture.md.

exerciseId.description: Canonical exercise id when referenceType is training_max or one_rep_max.

firstRunnableWeekIndex.description: The earliest week in which this reference is consumed, as declared by the importer. The semantic validator cross-checks this against actual usage in programStructure.

supplied.description: True once the operator has provided the value during import. Activation requires every first-week reference to be supplied.

variantOf.description: Optional id of another programWeek within the SAME block. When set, this week is an alternate of variantOf; the runtime presents the base week and all its variants as a one-of choice and only the chosen week is executed. Chain depth is 1 (the target must not itself have variantOf). Resources that use this field MUST declare schemaVersion >= 2; variant-unaware loaders must reject schemaVersion 1 resources only.

variantLabel.description: Operator-facing short label distinguishing this variant within its group (e.g. 'A', 'B', 'heavy'). Required on every member of a multi-member variant group; labels within a group must be unique.

perSide.description: True for per-side prescriptions (e.g. single-arm dumbbell rows).

restSecondsHint.description: Rest hint in seconds. When restMaxSecondsHint is also present, this is the lower bound of a range; otherwise a single recommended rest. Requires schemaVersion >= 3 to be the lower bound of a range.

restMaxSecondsHint.description: Optional upper bound for the rest range. Requires restSecondsHint to also be present (dependentRequired). Requires schemaVersion >= 3.

warmupSetCount.description: Optional structured count of source-prescribed warm-up sets when the source does not specify per-set loads or reps. Runtime fills in the actual warm-up scheme. Requires schemaVersion >= 3.

percent.description: Single-value percent of the referenced max. Mutually exclusive with percentMin/percentMax.

percentMin.description: Lower bound of a percent range (e.g. 75 in '75-80%'). Requires schemaVersion >= 3 and must be paired with percentMax.

percentMax.description: Upper bound of a percent range. Requires schemaVersion >= 3 and must be paired with percentMin and strictly greater than percentMin.

parameters.description: Open object. Phase 2 keeps this extensible because progression-rule introspection lives in the Android program runner (Phase 4/6).
